Criteria: Critical fire weather conditions imminent or occurring (combination of high winds, low RH, and dry fuels)
Conditions favorable for the development of critical fire weather within 24 to 72 hours
Rapid rate of spread, intense crowning, spotting, or fire whirls present, compromising safety
Immediate threat to life and property. An explicit command to clear an area immediately.
High probability of threat to life and safety. Prepare to clear the area at a moment's notice.
Conditions require staying indoors or restricted movement due to fire path shifts or heavy active smoke zones.
PM2.5 concentrations pose an emergency condition; everyone is highly likely to experience serious health effects.
Significant risk of respiratory irritation and cardiovascular stress for the general population.
Active children, adults, and people with respiratory diseases should avoid prolonged outdoor exertion.
Members of sensitive groups may experience health effects; general public is less likely to be affected.
Fires start quickly, spread furiously, and are very difficult to control. Direct attack rarely successful.
Fires start easily from all causes and spread rapidly with high intensity. Spotting is a constant threat.
All fine dead fuels ignite readily and fires start easily. Spread can become rapid.
Fires can start from most accidental causes, but spread is moderate. Control is relatively easy.
Fuels do not ignite readily. Spreads slowly. Low danger level.
